_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The linter is now clearer.

The linter ignores front matter, code blocks, inline code, and "See also" sections. Multi-word phrases match case-insensitively; single words match case-sensitively with word boundaries. Self-references are skipped. The warning on line 16 of second-brain-daily-workflow.md matches the phrase "Second Brain".

The alias "Second Brain Workflow" matches the phrase "Second Brain workflow" in the body text. The regex finds word boundaries around both terms.

Line 18 matches the alias "The Archive" for The Archive Method. The fixes are to link "Second Brain" to Building a Second Brain and "the archive" to The Archive Method.

Self-references are skipped for the same note. A self-link would therefore not trigger the warning. The awkward double-link format is also impractical.

The note title is "Second Brain Daily Workflow", so it does not match either alias. The best fix is to link "Second Brain" to Building a Second Brain and "workflow" to The Second Brain Workflow.

The remaining fixes are now clear.

- **analog-capture-tools.md** line 16: link "trusted inbox" to The Trusted Inbox.
- **bullet-journaling.md** line 18: link "second brain" to Building a Second Brain.
- **context-aware-capture.md** line 16: the single word "Capture" matches the title case-sensitively.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The linter checks whether a wikilink starts with the needle text.

The alias text is not the link title. For example, `[[Building a Second Brain|Second Brain]]` starts with "Building a Second Brain". The linter misses these links because it expects the alias at the start.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

The linter only treats a mention as linked when the wikilink target starts with the note title or alias — piped links like `[[Building a Second Brain|Second Brain]]` don't count. Switching to alias-first targets.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
All 12 warnings from the selected terminal output are now fixed.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Fixed all 12 unlinked-mention warnings from your build output by adding wikilinks in the note body prose.

The linter only counts a mention as linked when the wikilink **target** starts with the note title or alias — so `[[Building a Second Brain|Second Brain]]` still triggers a warning. Alias-first targets like `[[Second Brain]]` or `[[Trusted Inbox|trusted inbox]]` satisfy it.

| File | Fix |
|------|-----|
| `second-brain-daily-workflow.md` | `[[Second Brain]]`, `[[Second Brain Workflow\|workflow]]`, `[[The Archive\|the archive]]` |
| `analog-capture-tools.md` | `[[Trusted Inbox\|trusted inbox]]` |
| `bullet-journaling.md` | `[[Second Brain\|second brain]]` |
| `context-aware-capture.md` | `[[Capture]] the why` |
| `daily-notes.md` | `[[The Archive\|the archive]]` |
| `evergreen-vs-fleeting-notes.md` | `[[Evergreen Notes\|Evergreen notes]]` |
| `slip-box-history.md` | `([[Zettelkasten]])` |
| `the-12-week-year.md` | `[[Archive Method\|archive]]` (avoids substring false positive) |
| `the-second-brain-workflow.md` | `[[Second Brain]] workflow` |
| `visual-thinking.md` | `[[Atomic Notes\|atomic notes]]` |

`npm run lint:notes` is clean for those. One unrelated warning remains: `"Daily Notes"` in `getting-started.md`. Say if you want that fixed too.

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Both warnings are resolved:

1. **`IsSet` on page pointer** — `notes/pages.html` was called with page objects (`$page`, `site.Home`, `.`) as well as dicts. `isset . "ExcludeKinds"` only works on maps, so Hugo warned on every page-object call. Switched to `with .ExcludeKinds`, which safely no-ops when the key isn't present.

2. **Unlinked "Daily Notes"** — In `getting-started.md`, linked the prose mention: `[[Daily Notes|Daily notes]] live under...`

`npm run lint:notes` and a full Hugo build now run clean with no warnings.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file_v2"><details>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The issue is clear. The partial `notes/pages.html` is called in two ways.

One call passes a dict. Another passes a page object. `with .ExcludeKinds` fails on pages because they lack that field. Normalizing all callers to pass dicts is cleaner than adding a type check inside the partial.

The partial only reads `site.RegularPages`, so passing page objects was unnecessary. Callers in `related-notes.html`, `link-registry.html`, and `backlinks.html` will be updated to pass dicts instead.

Using `reflect.IsMap` inside `pages.html` is the better fix. This safely handles both dict and page arguments without changing every caller.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Normalizing the partial: it only needs an optional dict, but some callers pass page objects. Using a type-safe check and updating those callers.


_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/layouts/_partials/notes/pages.html</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/layouts/_partials/notes/related-notes.html</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/layouts/_partials/notes/link-registry.html</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/layouts/_partials/notes/backlinks.html</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com &amp;&amp; hugo --gc 2&gt;&amp;1 | rg &#39;ERROR|WARN.*IsSet|unlinked mention|broken wikilink&#39; || echo &#34;Build clean&#34;</summary>
```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && hugo --gc 2>&1 | rg 'ERROR|WARN.*IsSet|unlinked mention|broken wikilink' || echo "Build clean"
```

```
Build clean

```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

Fixed. The `with .ExcludeKinds` change assumed the partial always received a dict, but three callers passed page objects (`$page`, `site.Home`, `.`). Hugo can't read `.ExcludeKinds` on a `page.Page`, which caused the rebuild failure on blog posts.

**Changes:**
- **`pages.html`** — guard with `reflect.IsMap .` before reading `ExcludeKinds`
- **`related-notes.html`**, **`link-registry.html`**, **`backlinks.html`** — pass `(dict)` instead of a page (the partial only uses `site.RegularPages` anyway)

Hugo build completes cleanly now, with no `IsSet` warning and no render errors.
